name: Cerrillo de la Buitrera Loop from Rute
name_original: Subida a El Canuto — circular desde Rute
description: This difficult 4.1-mile (6.5 km) jogging route in the Parque Natural de las Sierras Subbéticas offers panoramic views from El Canuto.
region_intro: Jogging around Rute offers diverse terrain within the Cordoban Subbética, characterized by mountainous landscapes and extensive olive groves. The region is situated within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, providing a network of trails with varying inclines and declines. Runners can explore routes along the scenic Iznájar Reservoir or through pine forests and historical sites. This setting provides a blend of natural beauty and challenging paths for all fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Rute The most popular jogging route is Iznajar and its Reservoir via the Colada de Valdearenas, a 4.3 miles (6.8 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views overlooking the expansive Iznájar Reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is Ascent to El Canuto — circular from Rute, a difficult 4.1 miles (6.5 km) path. This route involves a significant ascent, providing panoramic views of the surrounding mountainous landscape. Local runners also love the Viaducto del Alamedal — round trip on the Vía Verde del Aceite, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through a former railway line, often completed in about 30 minutes. This easy route is suitable for a relaxed run. Jogging around Rute is defined by mountainous terrain, olive groves, and the Iznájar Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Rute are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 5 reviews. Nearly 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Rute's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Rute?
A: Rute offers a wide selection of over 120 running routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These trails wind through the diverse landscapes of the Cordoban Subbética, including mountainous terrain and extensive olive groves.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Rute?
A: The running routes in Rute are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.8 stars from over 8 reviews. Nearly 1000 runners have explored the area, often praising the varied terrain and stunning natural beauty of the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park.

Q: Are there running options for different fitness levels?
A: Yes, Rute provides a good mix of running challenges. You'll find 6 easy routes perfect for a relaxed jog, 65 moderate trails for a balanced workout, and 55 difficult paths for those seeking a more intense experience with significant ascents.

Q: Can I find circular running routes around Rute?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Rute are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1288090761/ascent-to-el-canuto-circular-from-rute" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to El Canuto — circular from Rute&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, while the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31484052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to El Canuto loop from Rute&lt;/a&gt; provides an extended circular experience.

Q: Where can I find routes with scenic views in Rute?
A: For breathtaking vistas, consider routes that overlook the Iznájar Reservoir, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001004009/iznajar-and-its-reservoir-via-the-colada-de-valdearenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iznajar and its Reservoir via the Colada de Valdearenas&lt;/a&gt;. The mountainous landscapes of the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, especially from peaks like El Hacho, also offer spectacular panoramas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly jogging paths in Rute?
A: For a gentler experience suitable for families, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1286046953/viaducto-del-alamedal-round-trip-on-the-via-verde-del-aceite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viaducto del Alamedal — round trip on the Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5 km trail along a former railway line. Additionally, the Nuestra Señora del Carmen Park within Rute offers pleasant paths through gardens and green spaces, ideal for lighter jogs.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks I can see while running?
A: Yes, Rute's trails often combine natural beauty with historical intrigue. You can jog towards the ruins of Rute el Viejo, an ancient Muslim fortress, and the Torre del Canuto watchtower. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4050527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Río&lt;/a&gt;, a natural karst spring with landscaped gardens, is another beautiful spot to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Rute's running trails?
A: Rute's running trails feature diverse terrain, from the rugged, mountainous landscapes of the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park with varying inclines and declines, to gentler paths through olive groves and along the Iznájar Reservoir. You can also find routes through pine forests, such as those along the Pinar de la Sierra.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options for experienced runners?
A: Experienced runners looking for a challenge will find several long-distance options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31484700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morrón del Salvador loop from Rute&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 21.7 km route with significant elevation gain, perfect for a longer, more demanding run.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running routes in Rute?
A: Many of the natural trails and paths in the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park and surrounding areas are suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept under control. Always check local regulations for specific areas, especially within protected zones, but generally, the open countryside offers ample space for you and your canine companion.

Q: What are some less crowded spots for a peaceful run?
A: To avoid crowds, consider exploring some of the less-trafficked trails deeper within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park or the Pinar de la Sierra. These areas offer a serene environment for a peaceful run amidst nature, away from more popular viewpoints or town-adjacent paths.

Q: Are there any accessible running trails in Rute?
A: For more accessible running, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1286046953/viaducto-del-alamedal-round-trip-on-the-via-verde-del-aceite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viaducto del Alamedal — round trip on the Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; is an easy, relatively flat path along a former railway line, making it suitable for a wider range of abilities. The paths within Nuestra Señora del Carmen Park also offer gentle, accessible surfaces.
tips: Tower that is at the top of the town of Rute, where you can see the whole of the center and the spaces that are around it. The way up to the tower is beautiful and worth it!

faqs.question: How challenging is the Ascent to El Canuto — circular from Rute trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered difficult for jogging, requiring very good fitness due to its significant elevation gain of approximately 367 meters over 6.55 kilometers. For hiking, it's rated as moderate, still requiring good fitness. It's a popular choice for local runners seeking a demanding experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ascent to El Canuto trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a significant ascent with a mix of surfaces. While largely comprising paved sections, you will also encounter unpaved paths. The route traverses mountainous terrain and extensive olive groves, characteristic of the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park. Some parts include a 'cresta' (ridge) with minor climbs and descents.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Ascent to El Canuto route?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking panoramic views. The primary highlight is the Ascent to El Canuto, where a historic Moorish watchtower at Cerro del Canuto provides spectacular vistas. From here, you can see the town of Rute, the expansive Iznájar Reservoir, the Sierra Nevada, the Torcal de Antequera rock formation, and other scenic areas of southern Andalusia.

faqs.question: Where does the Ascent to El Canuto trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Rute. While specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, Rute is a town with various parking options. It's advisable to check local parking availability in Rute before your visit.

faqs.question: Is Ascent to El Canuto — circular from Rute a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same location in Rute, allowing you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to experience the Ascent to El Canuto trail?
faqs.answer: The region experiences hot summers, so spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the most pleasant times to visit. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, making the significant ascent more enjoyable and the views clearer. Winter can also be suitable, but be prepared for cooler temperatures.

faqs.question: Is the Ascent to El Canuto — circular from Rute trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally open, it's located within the Parque Natural de las Sierras Subbéticas. Dogs are typically allowed in natural parks but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always carry water for your dog, especially on challenging routes like this one.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to access the Ascent to El Canuto trail, or regulations within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to access this trail or the Parque Natural de las Sierras Subbéticas. However, as it's within a natural park, visitors are expected to follow general regulations such as staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and packing out all trash to preserve the environment.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 43% of this route overlaps with sections of the official trail known as Andalucía - North Route (Provincia de Córdoba).

faqs.question: What should I bring for the Ascent to El Canuto trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and elevation gain, essential items include plenty of water, appropriate footwear with good grip (trail running shoes or hiking boots), sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather. Snacks for energy are also recommended.

faqs.question: Is the Ascent to El Canuto trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Due to its 'difficult' rating for jogging and 'moderate' for hiking, with a significant elevation gain, this trail is generally not recommended for young children or families seeking an easy outing. It requires good fitness and stamina. Older, experienced, and fit children might manage it, but it's a challenging route.
